summary: path-addon performs import-time fetch-and-eval of remote code from anonymous paste host
details: On require('path-addon'), path.js performs a top-level fetch to a base64-obfuscated URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/RFW6I — an anonymous, mutable JSON paste service) and passes the returned `data.content` directly to eval(). This grants whoever controls that paste arbitrary remote code execution in every consumer process that imports the package, with no integrity check, no version pin, and no publisher relationship to the destination. The URL is hidden via atob() to evade trivial string scanners. The package is also a typosquat: package.json describes itself as 'Node.js path module' and the README claims to be 'an exact copy of the NodeJS path module', re-exporting the historical path polyfill API as cover for the embedded remote-eval payload. Author metadata is empty.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
